title: A rat model of contrast-induced acute kidney injury following intra-arterial contrast administration
author: Zhiqing Wang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.z6bf9an
materials: Body weight of each rat was measure.

steps.title: Rats were deprived of water for 48 h ours。
steps.instruction: Rats were deprived of water for 48 h ours。

steps.title: Deeply anaesthetized was conducted by pentobarbital sodium (40 mg/kg, i
steps.instruction: Deeply anaesthetized was conducted by pentobarbital sodium (40 mg/kg, i.p. ).

steps.title: The right femoral vein and carotid...
steps.instruction: The right femoral vein and carotid artery were both cannulated by short peripheral catheters which were widely used in the nursing practice (24 G×21 mm).

steps.title: A baseline blood sample was drawn...
steps.instruction: A baseline blood sample was drawn (1 ml) from the carotid artery for determination of SCr.

steps.title: Indomethacin was dissolved in phosphate buffer (pH 8
steps.instruction: Indomethacin was dissolved in phosphate buffer (pH 8.4) at a concentration of 5mg/ml, then administrated intravenously at the dose of 10 mg/kg.

steps.title: After 15 min, L-NAME (dissolved in 0
steps.instruction: After 15 min, L-NAME (dissolved in 0.9% normal saline at a concentration of 10mg/ml) was administrated intravenously at the dose of 10 mg/kg.

steps.title: After another 15 min, these rats...
steps.instruction: After another 15 min, these rats were randomized to receive iopromide (CI-AKI group) or normal saline (Control group) at the dose of 7.8 ml/kg via the carotid artery cannulation over a time course of 5 min.

steps.title: The rats were then allowed to...
steps.instruction: The rats were then allowed to recover in individual cages with free access to tap water and standard chow.

steps.title: At 12 hours post CM injection,...
steps.instruction: At 12 hours post CM injection, the rats were deeply anaesthetized by pentobarbital sodium (40 mg/kg, i.p.).

steps.title: A second set of arterial blood...
steps.instruction: A second set of arterial blood sample was obtained from the ventral aorta.

steps.title: The blood cells were removed by perfusing with 0
steps.instruction: The blood cells were removed by perfusing with 0.9% normal saline through the bilateral renal arteries.

steps.title: Both kidneys were harvested and bisected longitudinally
steps.instruction: Both kidneys were harvested and bisected longitudinally. The right kidney, containing cortex and medulla, was fixed in 10 % neutral formalin liquid for histological analysis.

steps.title: The rats were euthanized with an...
steps.instruction: The rats were euthanized with an overdose of pentobarbital anesthesia (200 mg/kg, i.p.).

steps.title: Blood samples were centrifuged within 2...
steps.instruction: Blood samples were centrifuged within 2 hours at 1000 × g for 15 min.

steps.title: The levels of SCr were measured...
steps.instruction: The levels of SCr were measured at the hospital clinical laboratory by a standard method.

steps.title: The histological samples were embedded in...
steps.instruction: The histological samples were embedded in paraffin and sectioned at 3 to 4 μm thick.

steps.title: Deparaffinize each slide with xylene (repeated...
steps.instruction: Deparaffinize each slide with xylene (repeated 3 times, 2 min each).

steps.title: Hydrate tissue sections with gradient ethanol...
steps.instruction: Hydrate tissue sections with gradient ethanol (100% ethanol repeated 3 times, 1 min each; 80% ethanol for 1 min) to distilled water in a fume hood.

steps.title: Incubate the slides with hematoxylin solution...
steps.instruction: Incubate the slides with hematoxylin solution in a staining jar for 4 min, and then rinse with tap water for 2 min to remove the unbound dye from slides.

steps.title: Transfer the slides to 0
steps.instruction: Transfer the slides to 0.1% hydrochloric acid in alcohol for 30 sec. Then rinse with tap water for 1 min.

steps.title: Transfer the slides to 0
steps.instruction: Transfer the slides to 0.5% ammonia solution for 1 min until the background turns light blue. Then rinse with tap water for 1 min.

steps.title: Transfer the slides to 80% ethanol for 1 min
steps.instruction: Transfer the slides to 80% ethanol for 1 min.

steps.title: Incubate the slides with 1% eosin...
steps.instruction: Incubate the slides with 1% eosin in a staining jar for 50 sec, and then rinse with tap water to remove the unbound dye from slides.

steps.title: Dehydrate with 100% ethanol (repeated 3...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate with 100% ethanol (repeated 3 times, 1 min each) and clear in xylene (repeated 3 times, 1 min each).

steps.title: The slides were then mounted with...
steps.instruction: The slides were then mounted with resinous mounting medium and stored at room temperature.

steps.title: After being visualized by hematoxylin and...
steps.instruction: After being visualized by hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining , the slides of each sample were reviewed by light microscope .

steps.title: FTubular injury was graded by the...
steps.instruction: FTubular injury was graded by the Paller score, a semi-quantitative evaluation. For each kidney, 100 tubules from 10 highmagnification (×200) fields of the medulla and the outer medulla were scored. As expected, the administration of CM, following the pretreatment by indomethacin and L-NAME, induced remarkable renal dysfunction at 12 hours post-procedure. The average elevation of SCr was (59.9±23.0) % over baseline in the CI-AKI rats, comparing to (-10.5 ± 17.1) % in the control rats injected with normal saline (Fig 1, p < 0.05). The pronounced histopathological alterations in the tubular epithelial cells of the inner medulla and outer medulla were observed in the CI-AKI rats, including vacuole-like denaturation, nucleus pycnosis and apoptotic body formation (Fig 2A). Additionally, the mean Paller score for the contrast treated kidneys was 8.2 ± 0.9, whereas that for controls was 3.6 ± 1.2 (Fig 2B, p < 0.0 5), indicating of a greater extent of tubular injury after the intervention of contrast. Fig 1.The averageelevation of sCr in CI-AKI rats was (59.9±23.0) % over baseline, comparedwith (-10.5±17.1) % in controls (p < 0.001). Fig 2A. Contrast medium treatment induced representative histopathological changes in the extrarenal medulla of H&E-stained kidney sections. Fig 2B. The average Paller score was much higher in CI-AKI rats than controls (8.2 ± 0.9 vs. 3.6 ± 1.2, P < 0.05)
